title: wire type can't be assignment by always@(*) block?
body:
```verilog
module conflict(
    input clk,
    input a,
    input b,
    input e,
    output  reg [3:0] q,
    output [3:0] wire_q
);

    integer i;
    always@(posedge clk) begin
        for(i=0;i<4;i++) begin
            q[i] <= a&b ;
        end
    end
    // assign wire_q = {4{e}};
   always@(*) begin
        for(i=0;i<4;i++) begin
            wire_q[i] = e ;
        end
   end
endmodule
```
my code is above. The error messege is 
```
conflict.v:22: error: wire_q[i] is not a valid l-value in conflict.
conflict.v:7:      : wire_q[i] is declared here as wire.
Elaboration failed
[Compile Failed]
```
**my version is:**
`Icarus Verilog Preprocessor version 11.0 (devel) (s20150603-911-gd1d6c0f5-dirty)`
code_blocks:
conflict.v:22: error: wire_q[i] is not a valid l-value in conflict.
conflict.v:7:      : wire_q[i] is declared here as wire.
